methods.method_name: Breath Gas Sampling and Conditioning Procedure
methods.description: This method describes the physical setup and procedures for collecting, filtering, and conditioning breath gas samples for spectroscopic analysis, including options for real-time or batch processing, and ensuring stable temperature and pressure conditions within the gas cell.

steps.description: Select the desired sampling mode, either real-time or batch, controlled by a solenoid valve group.
steps.searchable_summary: Select sampling mode for breath gas

steps.description: If real-time mode is selected, sample exhaled breath gas directly through a replaceable mouthpiece connected to the gas inlet.
steps.searchable_summary: Sample exhaled breath gas in real-time mode

steps.description: If batch mode is selected, connect prepared breath collection bags (up to 12 channels) to the system and use the solenoid valve group for automatic processing.
steps.searchable_summary: Sample breath gas from collection bags in batch mode
inputs: Solenoid valve control signal (for channel selection and inlet)

steps.description: Filter the collected breath sample to remove water and particles.
steps.searchable_summary: Filter breath gas sample to remove water and particles

steps.description: Extract the filtered breath sample into the spectroscopic gas cell using a pump.
steps.searchable_summary: Pump filtered breath gas into gas cell

steps.description: Control the gas inlet and outlet flow rates using solenoid operated proportional valves to ensure a stable low pressure in the gas cell, regulated by a homemade digital PID algorithm based pressure controller receiving feedback from a pressure transducer.
steps.searchable_summary: Control gas cell pressure using PID controller and proportional valves

steps.description: Maintain a stable temperature in the insulation case surrounding the optical components and gas cell using a pair of thermoelectric coolers controlled by a homemade digital PID algorithm based temperature controller receiving feedback from a 10 kΩ thermistor mounted onto the gas cell.
steps.searchable_summary: Control gas cell temperature using PID controller and thermoelectric coolers

methods.method_name: Isotope Ratio Calculation from Absorption Spectra
methods.description: This method describes the theoretical basis and calculation steps to determine isotope ratios from laser direct absorption spectroscopy measurements using the Beer-Lambert law, integrated absorption areas, temperature-dependent transition intensities, and known isotopologue abundances.

steps.description: Acquire the transmitted laser intensity spectrum (IT(ν)) after passing through the gas sample in the cell and the incident laser intensity spectrum (I0(ν)) (baseline).
steps.searchable_summary: Acquire laser transmission and baseline spectra

steps.description: Calculate the absorption spectrum at each frequency ν using the Beer-Lambert law formula: -ln(IT(ν)/I0(ν)).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate absorption spectrum from transmission and baseline spectra

steps.description: Integrate the absorption spectrum over the frequency range corresponding to the spectral lines of the rare (r) and abundant (a) isotopologues to determine their respective integrated absorption areas (Ar and Aa).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate integrated absorption area for isotopologue spectral lines

steps.description: Obtain the temperature-dependent transition intensities Sr(T) and Sa(T) for the rare and abundant isotopologues. This may involve looking up values from databases or calculating them from reference values S(T0) and spectroscopic parameters (E", ν0) using the provided formula.
steps.searchable_summary: Get or calculate temperature-dependent transition intensities
inputs: Spectroscopic database/parameters (S(T0), E", ν0, Q(T0), Q(T))

steps.description: Obtain the known natural or reference isotopologue abundances nr and na.
Calculate the isotope ratio (ratio of number densities Nr/Na) using the formula: (Ar / (Sr(T) * nr * L)) / (Aa / (Sa(T) * na * L)), where L is the absorption path length.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate isotope ratio from integrated absorption areas and spectroscopic parameters
